entities.name: customers
entities.description: Customer accounts. One row per customer.

entities.name: orders
entities.description: Orders placed by customers. One row per order.

entities.name: customers
entities.description: Customer accounts. One row per customer.

example_queries.question: How many customers do we have?
example_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT id) AS customer_count FROM customers

entities.name: orders
entities.description: Orders placed by customers. One row per order.

segments.name: current_quarter
segments.description: Orders in the current calendar quarter.

example_queries.question: Total revenue this quarter
example_queries.sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed' AND created_at >= date_trunc('quarter', CURRENT_DATE)

hints: Revenue and average order value use status = 'completed' only; exclude cancelled, draft, and refunded rows unless the question is about refunds.
Monthly trends: GROUP BY date_trunc('month', created_at).
Top-N customer questions: GROUP BY customer_id, ORDER BY the metric DESC, LIMIT N.
Overdue balance: pending or processing orders with due_date < CURRENT_DATE; use SUM(total_amount - amount_paid).
Calendar month filters: created_at >= date_trunc('month', ...) AND created_at < date_trunc('month', ...) + interval '1 month'.
Previous calendar quarter: created_at >= date_trunc('quarter', CURRENT_DATE) - interval '3 months' AND created_at < date_trunc('quarter', CURRENT_DATE).
If a status value does not exist in the semantic layer, investigate (e.g. run a broader query) before concluding there is no data.
